mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, prompting equity markets to price in higher discount rates and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% in >40% of cases despite the signal, or CPIAUCSL's predictive power fails in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.2) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening phase (UNRATE < 4%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals elevated wage pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days due to inflationary risk premiums.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in tight labor market scenarios exceed 1.2% in >50% of cases, or interaction term significance drops below p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening manufacturing activity, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through reduced corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: INDPRO's 5-day predictive power fails (Rank IC < 0.02) in 2-year rolling windows or SPX returns show no correlation with INDPRO declines.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals strong aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: Interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows no significant impact on SPX returns (p > 0.15) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening monetary policy (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days due to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in this regime exceed 2.5% in >35% of cases, or interaction term significance drops below p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 0.2pp change) signals labor market overheating, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through increased consumer spending and corporate pricing power.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in low-unemployment scenarios fall below 0.5% in >40% of cases, or UNRATE's predictive power fails (p > 0.1).
